title: Magical Weeknight Spanish Fish Tacos
description: A quick and flavorful Spanish-inspired dish featuring crispy hard taco shells, perfect for a weeknight dinner. Pair with your favorite Spanish wine for an authentic touch.

ingredients.name: Cod Fillets
ingredients.notes: Fresh or frozen cod fillets work well for this recipe.

ingredients.name: Lemon Juice
ingredients.notes: Freshly squeezed lemon juice adds brightness to the dish.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince the garlic before adding it to the pan.

ingredients.name: Smoked Paprika
ingredients.notes: Smoked paprika adds a deep, smoky flavor to the dish.

ingredients.name: Chopped Cilantro
ingredients.notes: Fresh cilantro adds a fresh, herbaceous flavor to the dish.

ingredients.name: Sour Cream
ingredients.notes: Optional: add a dollop of sour cream on top of each taco for extra creaminess.

ingredients.name: Shredded Lettuce
ingredients.notes: Add shredded lettuce for extra crunch and freshness.

instructions: Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Place the cod fillets on the prepared baking sheet.
Drizzle the cod with olive oil and sprinkle with salt, black pepper, and smoked paprika.
Bake the cod in the preheated oven for 8-10 minutes, or until cooked through.
While the cod is baking, heat the panko breadcrumbs in a dry skillet over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, or until fragrant.
Crumble the cooked cod into small pieces and add it to the skillet with the panko breadcrumbs.
Stir to combine and cook for 1-2 minutes, or until the cod is coated in the breadcrumbs.
Warm the hard taco shells according to package instructions.
Assemble the tacos by spooning the cod mixture into the warm taco shells.
Top each taco with a dollop of sour cream, shredded lettuce, and chopped cilantro.
